page: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a shared-CPU instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is also included.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core stress-ng performance and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the top tier. However, multi-core stress-ng performance, Passmark CPU scores, and database operations fall into the bottom 25 percent, reflecting the constraints of the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth for large blocks and static web serving throughput perform at average levels.

Given its shared CPU model, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require strong bursty single-core performance but do not demand sustained multi-core execution. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, basic compression tasks, development environments, and low-traffic applications. It is less suitable for intensive database operations or multi-threaded processing due to resource sharing.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a shared-CPU instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, equipped with an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data highlights excellent single-threaded performance, with single-core stress-ng and compression tasks ranking in the top 10 percent. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET throughput are limited, falling into the bottom 25 percent due to the shared resource model. This instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads prioritizing single-thread speed over sustained multi-core power, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic utility scripts.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a shared-CPU instance featuring an AMD EPYC-Turin processor, 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 120 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-threaded performance for budget-friendly workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a shared-CPU instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 120 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for budget-friendly, single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared instance combining top-tier single-threaded performance with local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation suitable for budget-conscious deployments
Best fit for lightweight web serving, development, and basic utility tasks
